expectations.text: Notes strict priority prefers the smaller (constant -> scalar) step first (the coach's own framing, not just an echo of the prompt token)
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: 'TPP orders transformations simple-to-complex so you pick the highest-priority one that turns the current red test green. constant -> scalar outranks scalar -> array.'

expectations.text: ALLOWS the reasoned deviation to (scalar -> array) as a heuristic with a stated reason -- does NOT rigidly refuse ('no, take the higher-priority move')
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: Rigid framing; the only 'legitimate exception' is when a scalar 'genuinely cannot make [the current test] pass' (= correct transformation, not a deviation). Never treats TPP as a heuristic permitting a reasoned foresight-based jump.

expectations.text: Coach, don't drive (no edit / no test run)
